task_completion.description: Open the Electrical Engineering Calculations app
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Electrical Engineering Calculations app is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the battery calculation section
task_completion.visual_evidence: A battery calculator or battery life/charging screen is open within the app.

task_completion.description: Input the battery capacity, charging current, and efficiency/type
task_completion.visual_evidence: The input fields show '200' for capacity (Ah), '25' for current (A), and either '85%' for efficiency or 'Lead Acid' selected.

task_completion.description: Calculate and display the estimated charging time
task_completion.visual_evidence: The app displays a calculated result for the charging time, which should be a value between 7.5 and 11.5 hours.

task_completion.description: Save a screenshot of the result screen to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal command (e.g., screencap -p /sdcard/tasks/charge_time_screenshot.png) or file manager action shows the screenshot being saved to /sdcard/tasks/charge_time_screenshot.png.

task_completion.description: Save the numerical result in hours to the specified text file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal command (e.g., echo) or text editor shows the numerical result being saved to /sdcard/tasks/charge_time_result.txt.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual Electrical Engineering Calculations app GUI to compute the result
integrity.visual_evidence: The calculation is performed within the graphical interface of the app, not via a Python script, terminal calculator, or web browser.

integrity.description: The saved screenshot is a genuine capture of the app's result screen
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshot command captures the actual screen where the app displays the calculated result, rather than a blank screen or unrelated app.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the app to hardcode a guessed result
integrity.visual_evidence: The value saved to the text file matches the exact result displayed on the app's calculation screen, proving it was derived from the app.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected charging time for a 200 Ah battery using a 25 A charger at 85% efficiency is between 7.5 and 11.5 hours (specifically around 8.89 to 9.41 hours). The agent must use the Electrical Engineering Calculations app to find this result. Note: If the app lacks a specific 'Battery Charging Time' calculator, the agent may use a related battery calculator within the app to derive the time. The final text file must contain the numerical result, and the screenshot must capture the app displaying this result.
